mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various agent frameworks. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and potential learning curve for new users.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface which is required to interact with the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using the Kagenti CLI with the specified version and node count.

```bash

kagenti create cluster --name kagenti-cluster --version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your desired region.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kagenti kubeconfig --cluster kagenti-cluster

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaler configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

kagenti delete cluster --name kagenti-cluster

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command deletes the cluster and all resources.
> This command deletes the namespace associated with Kagenti.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step upgrades the Kagenti installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kagenti upgrade --version v0.4.0-alpha.1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> This command allows the node to accept new pods again.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or quota in your cloud provider.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud provider's IAM permissions and resource quotas.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Run 'kagenti list clusters' to check the status of your clusters.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to apply autoscaler.yaml'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is set up correctly and that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl drain' fails with 'node not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified node may not exist or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the node name with 'kubectl get nodes' and try again.

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with core functionalities for deploying AI agents.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes environment set up with the necessary resources.
